checks.age25NotLetIn: The person is 25 — they should have been let in, but weren't.
checks.age18LetIn: The person is 18 — they should not have been let in, but were.
checks.age21NotLetIn: The person is 21 — they should have been let in, but weren't.
checks.age20LetIn: The person is exactly 20 — not over 20, so they should not have been let in, but were.

checkAge.name: Check the person's age
checkAge.description: Get the person's age and check if they're over 20. If they are, let them in.

age25.name: Age 25
age25.description: The person is 25 years old — they should be let in.

age18.name: Age 18
age18.description: The person is 18 years old — they should not be let in.

age21.name: Age 21
age21.description: The person is 21 years old — just above 20, they should be let in.

age20.name: Age 20
age20.description: The person is exactly 20 — not over 20, so they should not be let in.

askAge.question: How do I know their age?
askAge.answer: Use the `askAge()` function. It will return a different value in each scenario.

ifStatement.question: How do I vary what I do?
ifStatement.answer: Use an `if` statement - check the concept if you need more help!

askAge.description: Returns the **age** of the person waiting outside.
